sourceQuestion:
Let A and B be sets and let A' and B' denote the complements of the sets A and B. The set (A - B) ∪ (B - A) ∪ (A ∩ B) is equal to:
A. A ∪ B
B. A' ∪ B'
C. A ∩ B
D. A' ∩ B'

parsed.stem: Let A and B be sets and let A' and B' denote the complements of the sets A and B. The set (A - B) ∪ (B -

options.text: is equal to: A. A ∪ B B. A' ∪ B'

sourceQuestion:
In a room containing 18 people, there are 15 people who speak English, 13 people who speak Hindi and 11 people who speak Kannada. 9 persons speak both English and Hindi, 9 persons speak both Hindi and Kannada whereas 9 persons speak both Kannada and English. How many speak all three languages?
A. 9
B. 8
C. 7
D. 6

parsed.stem: In a room containing 18 people, there are 15 people who speak English, 13 people who speak Hindi and 11 people who speak Kannada. 9 persons speak both English and Hindi, 9 persons speak both Hindi and Kannada whereas 9 persons speak both Kannada and English. How many speak all three languages?

sourceQuestion:
Let P(S) denote the power set of set S. Which of the following is always true?

A. P(S) ∩ P(T) ⊆ P(S ∩ T)
B. P(S) ∩ P(T) = P(S ∩ T)
C. P(S) ∩ P(T) ⊃ P(S ∩ T)
D. P(S) ∩ P(T) - P(S ∩ T) = {Ø}

parsed.stem: Let P(S) denote the power set of set S. Which of the following is always true?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following statements:

S_1: There exist infinite sets A, B, C such that A ∩ (B ∪ C) is finite.
S_2: There exist two irrational numbers x and y such that (x + y) is rational.

Which of the following is true about S_1 and S_2?

A. Only S_1 is correct
B. Only S_2 is correct
C. Both S_1 and S_2 are correct
D. None of S_1 and S_2 is correct
sourceAnswer: C. Both S_1 and S_2 are correct

parsed.stem:
Consider the following statements:

S_1: There exist infinite sets A, B, C such that A ∩ (B ∪ C) is finite.
S_2: There exist two irrational numbers x and y such that (x + y) is rational.

Which of the following is true about S_1 and S_2?

options.text: Both S_1 and S_2 are correct
None of S_1 and S_2 is correct

sourceQuestion:
Let A, B and C be non-empty sets and let X = (A - B) - C and Y = (A - C) - (B - C). Which one of the following is TRUE?

A. X = Y
B. X ⊂ Y
C. Y ⊂ X
D. None of these

parsed.stem: Let A, B and C be non-empty sets and let X = (A - B) - C and Y = (A - C) - (B - C). Which one of the following is TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
If P, Q, R are subsets of the universal set U, then (P ∩ Q ∩ R) ∪ (P^c ∩ Q ∩ R) ∪ Q^c ∪ R^c equals:

A. P^c ∪ Q^c ∪ R^c
B. P ∩ (Q ∪ R)
C. U
D. P ∪ (Q ∩ R)

parsed.stem: If P, Q, R are subsets of the universal set U, then (P ∩ Q ∩ R) ∪ (P^c ∩ Q ∩ R) ∪ Q^c ∪ R^c equals:

sourceQuestion:
Given a set of elements N = {1, 2, ..., n} and two arbitrary subsets A subset_of N and B subset_of N, how many of the n! permutations pi from N to N satisfy min(pi(A)) = min(pi(B)), where min(S) is the smallest integer in the set of integers S, and pi(S) is the set of integers obtained by applying permutation pi to each element of S?

A. (n - |A union B|)! * |B|!
B. |A|^n * |B|^n
C. n! / |A union B|
D. n! / (|A| * |B|)

parsed.stem: Given a set of elements N = {1, 2, ..., n} and two arbitrary subsets A subset_of N and B subset_of N, how many of the n! permutations pi from N to N satisfy min(pi(A)) = min(pi(B)), where min(S) is the smallest integer in the set of integers S, and pi(S) is the set of integers obtained by applying permutation pi to each element of S?

sourceQuestion:
If P, Q, R are subsets of the universal set U, then

(P intersection Q) union (P intersection Q') union (P' intersection Q') union (Q' intersection R')

is equal to:

A. Q' union R'
B. P union Q' union R'
C. P' union Q'
D. U

parsed.stem:
If P, Q, R are subsets of the universal set U, then

(P intersection Q) union (P intersection Q') union (P' intersection Q') union (Q' intersection R')

is equal to:

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following relation on subsets of the set S of integers between 1 and 2014. For two distinct subsets U and V of S we say U < V if the minimum element in the symmetric difference of the two sets is in U.

Consider the following two statements:
- S1: There is a subset of S that is larger than every other subset.
- S2: There is a subset of S that is smaller than every other subset.

Which one of the following is CORRECT?

A. Only I (S1 is true)
B. Both S1 and S2 are false
C. S1 is false and S2 is true
D. Neither S1 nor S2 is true

parsed.stem:
Consider the following relation on subsets of the set S of integers between 1 and 2014. For two distinct subsets U and V of S we say U < V if the minimum element in the symmetric difference of the two sets is in U.

Consider the following two statements:
- S1: There is a subset of S that is larger than every other subset.
- S2: There is a subset of S that is smaller than every other subset.

Which one of the following is CORRECT?

options.text: Both S1 and S2 are false
S1 is false and S2 is true
Neither S1 nor S2 is true

sourceQuestion:
For a set A, the power set of A is denoted by 2^A. If A = {{0,1}, {1}}, which of the following options are TRUE?

I. empty_set subset_of 2^A
II. {1} subset_of 2^A
III. {{1}} in 2^A
IV. {{0,1}} subset_of 2^A

A. I and III only
B. II and III only
C. I, II and III only
D. I, II and IV only
sourceAnswer: C. I, II and III only

parsed.stem:
For a set A, the power set of A is denoted by 2^A. If A = {{0,1}, {1}}, which of the following options are TRUE?

I. empty_set subset_of 2^A
II. {1} subset_of 2^A
III. {{1}} in 2^A
IV. {{0,1}} subset_of 2^A

sourceQuestion:
Suppose U is the power set of the set S = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}. For any T in U, let |T| denote the number of elements in T and T' denote the complement of T. For any T, R in U, let T\R be the set of all elements in T which are not in R. Which one of the following is true?

A. For any T, R in U, if |T| = |R|, then |T\R| = |T'\R'|
B. |X| < |X'| for all X in U such that |X| < 3
C. 2^|T| + 2^|R| = 2^|T union R| + 2^|T intersection R| for all T, R in U
D. For any T, R in U, |T\R| = |T| - |T intersection R|
sourceAnswer: D. For any T, R in U, |T\R| = |T| - |T intersection R|

parsed.stem: Suppose U is the power set of the set S = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}. For any T in U, let |T| denote the number of elements in T and T' denote the complement of T. For any T, R in U, let T\R be the set of all elements in T which are not in R. Which one of the following is true?

options.text: For any T, R in U, if |T| = |R|, then |T\R| = |T'\R'|
|X| < |X'| for all X in U such that |X| < 3
2^|T| + 2^|R| = 2^|T union R| + 2^|T intersection R| for all T, R in U
For any T, R in U, |T\R| = |T| - |T intersection R|

sourceQuestion:
Consider a set U of 23 different compounds in a chemistry lab. There is a subset S of U of 9 compounds, each of which reacts with exactly 3 compounds of U. Consider the following statements:

I. Each compound in U \ S reacts with an odd number of compounds.
II. At least one compound in U \ S reacts with an odd number of compounds.

Which one of the following is ALWAYS TRUE?

A. Only I
B. Only II
C. Neither I nor II
D. Both I and II

parsed.stem:
Consider a set U of 23 different compounds in a chemistry lab. There is a subset S of U of 9 compounds, each of which reacts with exactly 3 compounds of U. Consider the following statements:

I. Each compound in U \ S reacts with an odd number of compounds.
II. At least one compound in U \ S reacts with an odd number of compounds.

Which one of the following is ALWAYS TRUE?

sourceQuestion:
For two n-dimensional real vectors P and Q, the operation a(P, Q) is defined as follows:

a(P, Q) = sum_{i=1}^{n} (P_i * Q_i)

Let C be a set of 10-dimensional non-zero real vectors such that for every pair of distinct vectors P, Q in C, a(P, Q) = 0. What is the maximum cardinality possible for the set C?

A. 9
B. 10
C. 11
D. 100

parsed.stem:
For two n-dimensional real vectors P and Q, the operation a(P, Q) is defined as follows:

a(P, Q) = sum_{i=1}^{n} (P_i * Q_i)

Let C be a set of 10-dimensional non-zero real vectors such that for every pair of distinct vectors P, Q in C, a(P, Q) = 0. What is the maximum cardinality possible for the set C?

sourceQuestion:
In a class of 200 students, 125 students have taken Programming Language course, 85 students have taken Data Structures course, 65 students have taken Computer Organization course. 45 students have taken both Programming Language and Data Structures, 40 students have taken both Programming Language and Computer Organization, 30 students have taken both Data Structures and Computer Organization, and 15 students have taken all the three courses. How many students have not taken any of the three courses?

A. 15
B. 20
C. 25
D. 30

parsed.stem: In a class of 200 students, 125 students have taken Programming Language course, 85 students have taken Data Structures course, 65 students have taken Computer Organization course. 45 students have taken both Programming Language and Data Structures, 40 students have taken both Programming Language and Computer Organization, 30 students have taken both Data Structures and Computer Organization, and 15 students have taken all the three courses. How many students have not taken any of the three courses?

sourceQuestion:
Let A be a set with n elements. Let C be a collection of distinct subsets of A such that for any two subsets S_1 and S_2 in C, either S_1 ⊆ S_2 or S_2 ⊆ S_1. What is the maximum cardinality of C?

A. n - 1
B. n + 1
C. 2^(n-1) + 1
D. n!

parsed.stem: Let A be a set with n elements. Let C be a collection of distinct subsets of A such that for any two subsets S_1 and S_2 in C, either S_1 ⊆ S_2 or S_2 ⊆ S_1. What is the maximum cardinality of C?

sourceQuestion:
Let P, Q, and R be sets. Let delta denote the symmetric difference operator defined as P delta Q = (P ∪ Q) - (P ∩ Q). Using Venn diagrams, determine which of the following is/are TRUE?

I. P delta (Q ∩ R) = (P delta Q) ∩ (P delta R)
II. P delta (Q ∪ R) = (P delta Q) ∪ (P delta R)

A. I only
B. II only
C. Neither I nor II
D. Both I and II

parsed.stem:
Let P, Q, and R be sets. Let delta denote the symmetric difference operator defined as P delta Q = (P ∪ Q) - (P ∩ Q). Using Venn diagrams, determine which of the following is/are TRUE?

I. P delta (Q ∩ R) = (P delta Q) ∩ (P delta R)
II. P delta (Q ∪ R) = (P delta Q) ∪ (P delta R)

sourceQuestion:
What is the cardinality of the set of integers defined below?

X = {n | 1 <= n <= 123, n is not divisible by either 2, 3, or 5}

A. 30
B. 33
C. 37
D. 44

parsed.stem:
What is the cardinality of the set of integers defined below?

X = {n | 1 <= n <= 123, n is not divisible by either 2, 3, or 5}

sourceQuestion:
Two girls have picked 10 roses, 15 sunflowers and 15 daffodils. What is the number of ways they can divide the flowers among themselves?

A. 1638
B. 2100
C. 2640
D. None of the above

parsed.stem: Two girls have picked 10 roses, 15 sunflowers and 15 daffodils. What is the number of ways they can divide the flowers among themselves?

sourceQuestion:
How many 4-digit even numbers have all digits distinct?

A. 2240
B. 2296
C. 2030
D. 4536

parsed.stem: How many 4-digit even numbers have all digits distinct?

sourceQuestion:
Let A be a sequence of 8 distinct integers sorted in ascending order. How many distinct pairs of sequences B and C are there such that:
i. each is sorted in ascending order,
ii. B has 3 elements and C has 5 elements, and
iii. the result of merging B and C gives A?

A. 56
B. 2296
C. 2030
D. 256

parsed.stem:
Let A be a sequence of 8 distinct integers sorted in ascending order. How many distinct pairs of sequences B and C are there such that:
i. each is sorted in ascending order,
ii. B has 3 elements and C has 5 elements, and
iii. the result of merging B and C gives A?

sourceQuestion:
n couples are invited to a party with the condition that every husband should be accompanied by his wife. However, a wife need not be accompanied by her husband. The number of different gatherings possible at the party is:

A. 2^(3n) / C(2n, n)
B. 3^n
C. C(2n, n) / 2
D. 2^n * C(2n, n)

parsed.stem: n couples are invited to a party with the condition that every husband should be accompanied by his wife. However, a wife need not be accompanied by her husband. The number of different gatherings possible at the party is:

sourceQuestion:
Mala has a colouring book in which each English letter is drawn two times. She wants to paint each of these prints with one of k colours, such that the colour pairs used to colour any two letters are different. Both prints of a letter can also be coloured with the same colour. What is the minimum value of k such that Mala can paint all the letters?

A. 9
B. 8
C. 7
D. 6

parsed.stem: Mala has a colouring book in which each English letter is drawn two times. She wants to paint each of these prints with one of k colours, such that the colour pairs used to colour any two letters are different. Both prints of a letter can also be coloured with the same colour. What is the minimum value of k such that Mala can paint all the letters?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the function g = |x| in the interval [-1, 1]. In this interval, the function is

A. continuous and differentiable
B. continuous but not differentiable
C. differentiable but not continuous
D. neither continuous nor differentiable

parsed.stem: Consider the function g = |x| in the interval [-1, 1]. In this interval, the function is

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following two statements about the function f(x) = |x|:

P. f(x) is continuous for all real values of x.
Q. f(x) is differentiable for all real values of x.

Which of the following is TRUE?

A. P is true and Q is false.
B. P is false and Q is true.
C. Both P and Q are true.
D. Both P and Q are false.
sourceAnswer: A. P is true and Q is false.

parsed.stem:
Consider the following two statements about the function f(x) = |x|:

P. f(x) is continuous for all real values of x.
Q. f(x) is differentiable for all real values of x.

Which of the following is TRUE?

options.text: P is true and Q is false.
P is false and Q is true.
Both P and Q are true.
Both P and Q are false.

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following functions is continuous at x = 3?

A. f(x) = { 2,      if x = 3
           { x - 1, if x != 3

B. f(x) = { 4,      if x = 3
           { 8/(x-3), if x != 3

C. f(x) = { x + 3, if x <= 3
           { x - 4, if x != 3

D. f(x) = { 1/(x-3), if x != 3
sourceAnswer: A. f(x) = 2 if x=3, x-1 if x != 3

parsed.stem: Which one of the following functions is continuous at x = 3?

sourceQuestion:
A function f(x) is continuous in the interval [0, 3]. It is known that f(0) = -2 and f(1) = 1. Which one of the following statements must be true?

A. There exists a y in the interval (0, 1) such that f(y) = f(y + 1)
B. For every y in the interval (0, 1), f(y) >= f(y + 1)
C. The maximum value of the function in the interval (0, 2) is 1
D. There exists a y in the interval (0, 3) such that f(y) = -(f(2 - y))
sourceAnswer: A. There exists a y in the interval (0, 1) such that f(y) = f(y + 1)

parsed.stem: A function f(x) is continuous in the interval [0, 3]. It is known that f(0) = -2 and f(1) = 1. Which one of the following statements must be true?

options.text: There exists a y in the interval (0, 1) such that f(y) = f(y + 1)
For every y in the interval (0, 1), f(y) >= f(y + 1)
The maximum value of the function in the interval (0, 2) is 1
There exists a y in the interval (0, 3) such that f(y) = -(f(2 - y))

sourceQuestion:
Let f(r) = e^(-1) and A(t) denote the area of the region bounded by f(r) and the X-axis, when r varies from -1 to t. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

I.  f is not bounded in [-1, 1]
II. A is not bounded in [-1, 1]
III. A is nonzero and finite

A. II only
B. III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

parsed.stem:
Let f(r) = e^(-1) and A(t) denote the area of the region bounded by f(r) and the X-axis, when r varies from -1 to t. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

I.  f is not bounded in [-1, 1]
II. A is not bounded in [-1, 1]
III. A is nonzero and finite

sourceQuestion:
Let f : R -> R be a function. Note: R denotes the set of real numbers. The function is defined as:

f(x) = -x,             if x < -2
f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c,  if -2 <= x <= 2
f(x) = c,              if x > 2

Which ONE of the following choices gives the values of a, b, c that make the function f continuous and differentiable?

A. a = 1/2, b = 0, c = -1
B. b = 1/2, a = 0, c = 0
C. a = 1/2, b = -1, c = 0
D. a = 1/2, b = 1, c = -4

parsed.stem:
Let f : R -> R be a function. Note: R denotes the set of real numbers. The function is defined as:

f(x) = -x,             if x < -2
f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c,  if -2 <= x <= 2
f(x) = c,              if x > 2

Which ONE of the following choices gives the values of a, b, c that make the function f continuous and differentiable?

sourceQuestion:
The function y = |2 - 3x|

A. is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R except at x = 2/3
B. is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R except at x = 2/3
C. is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R
D. is continuous for all x in R except at x = 2/3 and differentiable for all x in R
sourceAnswer: C. is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R

parsed.stem: The function y = |2 - 3x|

options.text: is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R except at x = 2/3
is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R except at x = 2/3
is continuous for all x in R and differentiable for all x in R
is continuous for all x in R except at x = 2/3 and differentiable for all x in R

sourceQuestion:
Let f(x) be a continuous function from R to R such that

f(x) = 1 - f(2 - x)

Which one of the following options is the CORRECT value of int_0^2 f(x) dx?

A. 0
B. 1
C. 2
D. -1

parsed.stem:
Let f(x) be a continuous function from R to R such that

f(x) = 1 - f(2 - x)

Which one of the following options is the CORRECT value of int_0^2 f(x) dx?
